title: Concrete preview / advanced-preview release targets for all buildable products
revision_note: RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-ALL-BUILDABLE-001 aligns the concrete targets with the RELEASE-PREVIEW-ALL-PRODUCTS-001 policy decision: EVERY buildable product is a preview (or advanced-preview) RELEASE target. Lack of hardware proof blocks STABLE only and never blocks a preview artifact. FanRelay / FanPWM / FanDAC move from passive 'manual-candidate-only / not releasable' to PREVIEW release targets delivered via the 'manual-preview' lane; FanTRIAC moves from the 'blocked' lane to the 'advanced-manual-preview' lane (no longer blocked-from-preview; only the HW-005 buildability blocker prevents an actual cut). WebFlash one-click import stays a separate, controlled follow-up (gated by the fan-token guardrail + WebFlash warning UX); the preview ARTIFACT is releasable now. No target is promoted to stable, marked recommended/default/REQUIRED_CONFIG, or allowed to claim hardware / bench / compliance / verified-schematic evidence.
description: Concrete, CI-consumable preview / advanced-preview release-target manifest for every buildable Sense360 firmware target. Makes the eligibility policy in config/release-channel-policy.json (RELEASE-PREVIEW-ALL-PRODUCTS-001) actionable by recording, per target, the config string, buildable YAML path, channel tier + build channel, expected artifact name, the release-note warning text, WebFlash import eligibility, the delivery lane (webflash / manual-preview / advanced-manual-preview), the stable blocker, and any build blocker that prevents cutting a preview artifact now. This file is target metadata only: it publishes no firmware, adds no config/webflash-builds.json row, flips no config/product-catalog.json status, promotes nothing to stable, claims no bench evidence / compliance / hardware or build proof, and does not touch the WebFlash repo. config/webflash-builds.json remains the sole WebFlash release-eligibility source of truth; the manual-preview / advanced-manual-preview lanes are non-WebFlash preview delivery lanes for the fan / TRIAC targets. Read by tests/test_preview_release_targets.py and scripts/validate_preview_release_targets.py.

scope.notes: Concrete target metadata only. No esphome CLI was available in this environment, so no new compile/build proof is claimed; every target that cannot be cut now records an explicit build_blocker. Cutting an actual config/webflash-builds.json row remains a separate, proof-bearing, per-target step.

delivery_lanes.webflash: Preview / advanced-preview artifact published as a WebFlash-importable build: a config/webflash-builds.json row + products/webflash wrapper + recorded build proof, exposed behind the acknowledgement gate. config/webflash-builds.json stays the sole WebFlash release-eligibility source of truth.
delivery_lanes.manual-preview: Buildable PREVIEW artifact released to testers via the manual-preview lane (config/manual-firmware-artifacts.json). The lane produces the tester-facing preview .bin -- these are real preview RELEASE targets, not passive candidates. WebFlash one-click import stays gated (the fan-token guardrail keeps fan drivers out of config/webflash-builds.json) until the WebFlash warning UX supports fan preview exposure; that import is a separate, controlled follow-up. The preview artifact itself is releasable now; no durable GitHub Release / WebFlash row is cut in this PR.
delivery_lanes.advanced-manual-preview: Advanced (mains-risk) PREVIEW artifact lane for TRIAC. Same as manual-preview but requires the advanced mains-risk warning copy and competent-person manual install. Releasable as advanced-preview once the target is buildable end-to-end; WebFlash import stays gated behind the advanced acknowledgement UX (separate follow-up). NOT a 'blocked-from-preview' lane: a target here is preview-allowed in principle and only an explicit build_blocker (e.g. HW-005 buildability) can stop an actual cut.

warning_copy.preview: PREVIEW FIRMWARE — buildable and installable for testers only. This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and NOT a customer default. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. Flash at your own risk and expect to recover with the rescue/stable firmware.
warning_copy.advanced-preview: ADVANCED PREVIEW — MAINS-VOLTAGE RISK. This firmware drives mains-voltage hardware (e.g. TRIAC phase-control dimming) and is for competent persons performing a manual installation only. It is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and is NEVER a default. No bench evidence and no electrical-safety / compliance certification is claimed. Incorrect installation can cause fire, electric shock, or death. Do not install unless you are qualified to work on mains wiring.

targets.product: Bathroom PoE (VentIQ + RoomIQ) — Release-One stable baseline

webflash_import_eligibility.reason: Live Release-One stable; unchanged by this manifest. Stable promotion is out of scope here.

targets.notes: Baseline reference only. The stable channel is evidence-gated and is not modified by RELEASE-PREVIEW-TARGETS-ALL-PRODUCTS-001.

webflash_import_eligibility.reason: SELV LED-bearing sibling; already published on the preview channel in config/webflash-builds.json.

No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. Flash at your own risk and expect to recover with the rescue/stable firmware.

webflash_import_eligibility.reason: SELV AirIQ + RoomIQ combination; preview-eligible per RELEASE-PREVIEW-ALL-PRODUCTS-001. AirIQ/VentIQ remain mutually exclusive.

No esphome CLI was available to record build proof. A catalog preview entry + products/webflash wrapper + recorded build proof are required before a config/webflash-builds.json row is cut.

webflash_import_eligibility.reason: Smallest SELV RoomIQ-only PoE bundle; preview-eligible per RELEASE-PREVIEW-ALL-PRODUCTS-001 (lack of hardware proof blocks stable only).

targets.release_note_warning: PREVIEW FIRMWARE — buildable and installable for testers only. This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and NOT a customer default. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. Flash at your own risk and expect to recover with the rescue/stable firmware.
targets.build_blocker: Preview is ALLOWED: lack of S360-410 hardware proof blocks STABLE only, not preview (the catalog status=blocked is a stable/lifecycle gate, not a preview prohibition). What still blocks an actual WebFlash row today: no products/webflash wrapper YAML exists yet (buildable today only as the compile-only skeleton products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-roomiq.yaml) and no esphome CLI was available to record build proof. A products/webflash wrapper + recorded build proof are required before a config/webflash-builds.json row is cut. Stable remains blocked by S360-410 PoE-PSU schematic verification.
targets.notes: Preview-allowed by policy. The catalog status=blocked is a STABLE/lifecycle gate (S360-410), NOT a preview prohibition, so this is no longer recorded as 'blocked-from-preview'. Catalog status is intentionally NOT flipped here; a products/webflash wrapper + build proof are the remaining steps before a WebFlash preview row is cut.

webflash_import_eligibility.reason: SELV RoomIQ + LED ring; preview-eligible per RELEASE-PREVIEW-ALL-PRODUCTS-001. Shared firmware for the Living-room and Corridor bundles.

targets.build_blocker: No dedicated product or products/webflash wrapper YAML yet (shares the compile-only RoomIQ skeleton, which does not encode the LED token). A product + wrapper YAML and recorded build proof are required before a config/webflash-builds.json row is cut.
targets.notes: One buildable firmware (Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED) consumed by both the Living-room and Corridor room bundles.

webflash_import_eligibility.reason: Preview ARTIFACT is releasable now via the manual-preview lane (config/manual-firmware-artifacts.json) -- this is a real preview release target, not a passive candidate. WebFlash one-click import stays gated: the fan-token guardrail (scripts/list_release_targets.py refuses fan tokens; catalog webflash_build_matrix locked false) keeps fan drivers out of config/webflash-builds.json until the WebFlash warning UX supports fan preview exposure. That WebFlash import is a separate, controlled follow-up (WEBFLASH-RELAY-001 / WEBFLASH-PWM-001 / WEBFLASH-DAC-001), not a preview block.

No esphome CLI was available here, so no NEW build proof is claimed -- the lane reuses the existing validated-full-compile compile-only proof.

targets.notes: 0-10V DAC fan control. Buildable (validated-full-compile); enforces the FanDAC<->AirIQ mutex. Now a PREVIEW release target: preview artifact delivered via the manual-preview lane; WebFlash import stays gated by the fan-token guardrail until the WebFlash warning UX is ready. Not Cloudlift-ready; no compliance claim. Not stable, not recommended, not default.

webflash_import_eligibility.reason: Mains-voltage TRIAC phase control. Advanced-manual-preview lane: the advanced (mains-risk) preview artifact is delivered via the advanced-manual-preview lane once the target is buildable end-to-end. NOT blocked-from-preview by a lack of stable evidence -- the ONLY thing preventing a cut is the HW-005 buildability blocker (see build_blocker). WebFlash one-click import stays gated behind the advanced acknowledgement UX (separate follow-up: WF-IMPORT-TRIAC-001) and is not enabled here. Advanced-preview ceiling: never stable, never recommended, never a default, never a REQUIRED_CONFIG, never a customer-kit default.

targets.release_note_warning: ADVANCED PREVIEW — MAINS-VOLTAGE RISK. This firmware drives mains-voltage hardware (e.g. TRIAC phase-control dimming) and is for competent persons performing a manual installation only. It is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and is NEVER a default. No bench evidence and no electrical-safety / compliance certification is claimed. Incorrect installation can cause fire, electric shock, or death. Do not install unless you are qualified to work on mains wiring.
targets.stable_blocker: HW-005 (S360-320 schematic; GPIO5/GPIO6 collision; ac_dimmer cannot run across SX1509) + PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 + COMPLIANCE-001 mains-voltage review.
targets.build_blocker: HW-005 BUILDABILITY blocker (NOT a stable-evidence gate and NOT a preview-policy block): S360-320 schematic uncommitted; placeholder GPIO5/GPIO6 collide with RoomIQ J10 nets; ESPHome ac_dimmer cannot run across the SX1509 expander. The target is therefore not yet buildable end-to-end, so no advanced-preview artifact can be cut on the advanced-manual-preview lane until HW-005 resolves. Stable is additionally gated by PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 + COMPLIANCE-001 mains-voltage review.
targets.notes: Mains-voltage TRIAC. Advanced-preview ONLY, with mandatory mains-risk warning copy, delivered (once buildable) via the advanced-manual-preview lane. No longer classed as a 'blocked' delivery lane: it is preview-allowed in principle and only the HW-005 buildability blocker prevents an actual cut. Never stable, recommended, default, REQUIRED_CONFIG, or WebFlash-exposed in this PR.
